data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: Interface elements are sized and positioned based on their expected interaction frequency.
data.technical_spec: Larger click targets for common actions, aligned with user flow analysis.

data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: Minimizes decision time by reducing the number of choices presented to users.
data.technical_spec: Simplified navigation menus and product offerings.

data.title: Law of Proximity
data.description: Group related items together to create logical and intuitive navigation paths.
data.technical_spec: Consistent layout with clearly delineated sections.

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: Limits the amount of information presented at once to avoid cognitive overload.
data.technical_spec: Information chunking and progressive disclosure techniques.

data.title: Jakob's Law
data.description: Users spend more time on other sites, so Apple.com offers familiar navigation patterns.
data.technical_spec: Adopts well-established UX paradigms, design patterns recognizable to users.
